commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Codex prompt/projector logic for a one-shot pre-tool chat acknowledgement, preserves message-tool-only delivery through prompt guidance and automatic suppression, and changes verbose plan progress to steps-only text with tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature PR rather than a bug report. Source inspection verifies the proposed behavior path, but I did not run a live transport scenario in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No narrow code repair is currently indicated; the next action is contributor or Mantis real behavior proof plus maintainer acceptance of the reply-ordering change.

target.title: Announce tool plans in chat replies
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds Codex-runtime developer guidance so chat turns that will need tools first prepare a one- or two-sentence, chat-ready acknowledgement that is specific to the user's actual request and workspace voice.
- Sends that acknowledgement from the Codex projector before the corresponding plan progress event in automatic source-delivery turns, so the first visible chat message is the specific human acknowledgement.
- Preserves `sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "message_tool_only"`: automatic projector delivery is suppressed there, and Codex is instructed to use the `message` tool first with the same acknowledgement before continuing.
- Keeps verbose/full plan progress as a separate second message, but renders only the numbered plan steps there instead of repeating the acknowledgement.
- Keeps deterministic redacted fallback copy only for cases where Codex has not produced a usable plan explanation.

## Root Cause

Before this patch, OpenClaw could only build the pre-tool chat reply from deterministic fallback signals such as tool names or plan step labels. That made the first reply accurate about tool usage, but still too generic for real operator asks because the projector ignored the model's plan explanation and the Codex runtime prompt did not require a chat-ready, request-specific acknowledgement.

The first refinement exposed two additional delivery issues. Verbose chat sessions already rendered `explanation + numbered steps`, while the new acknowledgement path rendered the same explanation again at tool start; that produced duplicated start messages and the ordered user experience was backwards because verbose plan progress could arrive before the acknowledgement. Review also caught that automatic `onBlockReply` delivery must not bypass `message_tool_only`, where the message tool owns visible source delivery.

Inspected code evidence: `extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.ts` emitted plan events as soon as Codex sent `turn/plan/updated`, and `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` formatted verbose plan progress as the explanation followed by all numbered steps. The patch makes the projector deliver the acknowledgement before emitting the plan event for automatic delivery, keeps verbose plan progress steps-only, and gates automatic announcements out of message-tool-only turns.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: a tool-requiring chat request now gets one specific acknowledgement first, and only verbose/full sessions get a second plan-list message.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw temp worktree with Codex app-server projector and shared auto-reply dispatch tests; operator manually verified a live Telegram group-topic run with private identifiers redacted from this PR body; local gateway updated with this PR overlay via `oc-update --skip-codex`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: sent a tool-requiring Telegram group-topic request and observed one concise acknowledgement before tool progress; reran focused Vitest coverage for the Codex projector, Codex run attempt, Codex thread lifecycle, and shared auto-reply dispatcher; reran test type probe and changed-lanes; pushed head `868b9cbafee4575c76899ddd898ac692ea2ea247`; ran `oc-update --skip-codex` after pushing.

Evidence after fix: the Codex projector tests assert the Beszel/Uptime Kuma-style acknowledgement is sent before the plan event and is not sent again on first tool start; message-tool-only tests assert no automatic source reply is sent and replay metadata remains safe; streamed plan-delta tests assert the fallback announcement refreshes until the first tool starts. The auto-reply dispatcher tests assert verbose plan progress renders only `1. ...` step lines, skips explanation-only plan progress, and releases inbound dedupe if an unsent explanation-only plan update is followed by an error.

Observed result after fix: normal mode gets only the concise acknowledgement; verbose/full mode gets the acknowledgement first and a separate numbered plan-list message second; message-tool-only mode keeps visible delivery owned by the message tool.

What was not tested: no real Beszel or Uptime Kuma credentials/endpoints were exercised; live Telegram proof was operator-observed and redacted rather than attached as a screenshot or recording.

## Verification

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` — 2 files passed, 104 tests passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` — 4 files passed, 424 tests passed
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental false --pretty false`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/changed-lanes.mjs --json` — core, core-test, extension, and extension-test lanes
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch` — first run found two actionable findings; final rerun was clean with no accepted/actionable findings
- `oc-update --skip-codex` — completed after applying PR #84534 as an overlay; build, daemon reinstall, and restart completed. Local health/security checks still reported unrelated operator-environment warnings (gateway event-loop degraded under load and pre-existing local security audit warnings) that are not caused by this patch
